MONET 0.1.0
  • Reference

Kinship Matrix from Pedigree

Source: R/kinship_from_pedigree.r
kinship_from_pedigree.Rd

Generates a kinship matrix from a pedigree data frame. The matrix represents the relatedness between individuals based on shared parents.

kinship_from_pedigree(pedigree)

Arguments

pedigree

A data frame with three columns:

  • id: Individual IDs (unique for each individual).

  • sire: IDs of the sire (father); NA for founders.

  • dam: IDs of the dam (mother); NA for founders.

Value

A symmetric kinship matrix where rows and columns are labeled with individual IDs. Diagonal elements represent self-relatedness (1), and off-diagonal elements represent relatedness between individuals.

Details

The function computes kinship coefficients under the assumption of non-inbred founders. Half-sibling relationships (shared sire or dam) yield a coefficient of \(\frac{1}{4}\). If individuals share both parents (full siblings), their kinship coefficient is \(\frac{1}{4} + \frac{1}{4} = \frac{1}{2}\).

Note: This function uses kinship2::kinship() internally and converts the kinship coefficients to a relatedness matrix by multiplying by 2.

References

Lynch, M., & Walsh, B. (1998). Genetics and analysis of quantitative traits. Sinauer Associates.

Author

Isabela do O isabela.doo@unil.ch

Examples

# Create a simple pedigree
pedigree <- data.frame(
  id = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),
  sire = c(NA, NA, "A", "A"),
  dam = c(NA, NA, "B", "B")
)

# Calculate kinship matrix
kinship_matrix <- kinship_from_pedigree(pedigree)
#> Error in kinship_from_pedigree(pedigree): could not find function "kinship_from_pedigree"

Contents

Developed by Isabela do O, Oscar E Gaggiotti, Pierre de Villemereuil, Jerome Goudet.
